pages.title: brief about me
pages.text: this article is also available in 中文 who am I#Hi there, my name is MilkBottle. Nice to meet you!As you can tell from language I use, I’m currently a college student in China. why started writing blog#This question dated back to 2020, with COVID-19 virus spreading across China, I had to study at home for a whole semester. And during that period I build my own AIO server which I later used to host a GHOST blog system on it. After frequently sharing my markdown course note with friends, I thought now I have a server, building a docker-base blog system should be quite easy and cool. And it turned out I enjoy sharing my thought about life and some IT stuff with others. why change into this carrier#To be honest, I haven’t try much ways to host my blog the reason I switch from GHOST to Hexo is some of my note include mathjax which is poorly supported on GHOST and Hexo’s static makes it more flexible.

pages.title: 关于我
pages.text: 本文同时提供以下语言的翻译： English 我是谁#你好呀，我是奶瓶。很高兴能认识你！我目前是一个计算机科学在读的本科生 为啥开始写博客的呢# .heimu { color: #000; background-color: #000; } .heimu:hover { color: #fff; } emmm, 这个故事要从一只蝙蝠说起（误， 2020年的时候，因为疫情在家上了一个学期课，期间就闲得无聊撸了一台all in one 服务器放在家里。然后后来同学经常会找我要我上课的笔记啥的。我就寻思，既然有服务器了，搭个docker的博客系统也不是啥麻烦事吧 还能装逼（后来发现并不能， 然后我就弄了个 GHOST 的博客系统。慢慢的我发现自己还蛮喜欢在把一些生活相关，科技相关的东西分享给大家的。 为啥换成Hexo了#说实话，我没啥资格评判哪个博客系统好用，毕竟就用过俩。一开始考虑的时候考虑的GHOST和wordpress，然后觉得wordpress烂大街了不够帅虽然免费但是不是开源的。然后选择了GHOST，GHOST的操作挺易懂的，docker一部署剩下的就纯web后台了。但是难受的地方是我有些笔记里面有用mathjax写一些公式，GHOST不能渲染，也没找到相关的插件。后面就换成了Hexo，markdown支持更好了然后还方便部署在不同的地方。

posts.title: SQL learning
posts.text: Why (Structure Query Language)SQL? High level Highly compatible key & superkey & foreign key Primary Key Unique Key Candidate Key Alternate Key Composite Key Super Key Foreign Key what is a key foreign key constraint integrity constraint catgorize L R LR N DDL table schema table view & temporary table DML insert delete update QL commands SELECT WHERE AS IN = ANY LIKE BETWEEN ORDER BY Order the result set by the value of a specific column When the specific column has same value, a secondary column will be helpful INSERT Add a complete new row to the table Add a partial row in to the table UPDATE DELETE Advanced SQL ROUND list set bag commands DISTINCT GROUP BY HAVING SUBSTRING() || or CONCAT DAYDIFF() (MySQL only) INTO INSERT INTO LIMIT INTERSECT UNION ALL problems when using time zone not correct 自学网站 https://sqlzoo.net/ Why (Structure Query Language)SQL?#High level#As a high level language you only need to care what data you want from the DBS rather then how I’m going to get it or what algorithm I’m going to use. Highly compatible#With only have to write what you need in SQL query and let DBS to decide how to get the results. Scientists can add new algorithm in to the DBS. And you don’t have to make any change to your SQL queries. key & superkey & foreign key#Primary Key#Primary Key is a field that can be used to identify all the tuples uniquely in the database. Only one of the columns can be declared as a primary key. A Primary Key can not have a NULL value. Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_id” is the Primary Key as it can identify all the row uniquely from the table. Unique Key#Unique Key can be a field or set of fields that can be used to uniquely identify the tuple from the database. One or more fields can be declared as a unique Key. The unique Key column can also hold the NULL value. Use of Unique Key improves the performance of data retrieval. It makes searching for records from the database much more faster & efficient. Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Unique Key as it can allow one value as a NULL in the column Candidate Key#Candidate Key can be a column or group of columns that can qualify for the Unique Key. Every table has at least one Candidate Key. A table may have one or more Candidate Key. Each Candidate Key can work as a Primary Key if required in certain scenarios. Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_id”, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Candidate Key as it can identify all the row uniquely from the table. These columns also qualify the criteria to be a Primary Key. Alternate Key#Alternate Key is that Key which can be used as a Primary Key if required. Alternate Key also qualifies to be a Primary Key but for the time being, It is not the Primary Key. Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Alternate Key as both of the columns can be a Primary Key but not yet selected for the Primary Key. Composite Key#Composite Key is also known as Compound Key / Concatenated Key. Composite Key refers to a group of two or more columns that can be used to identify a tuple from the table uniquely. A group of the column in combination with each other can identify a row uniquely but a single column of that group doesn’t promise to identify the row uniquely. Example: In the above given relational table i.e. Order Table, “cust_id”, “order_month_year” group of these columns used in combination to identify the tuple uniquely in the Order Table. The individual column of this table is not able to identify the tuple uniquely from the Order table. Super Key#Super Key is a combination of columns, each column of the table remains dependent on it. Super Key may have some more columns in the group which may or may not be necessary to identify the tuple uniquely from the table. Candidate Key is the subset of the Super Key. Candidate Key is also known as minimal Super Key. Example: In the above given relational table, Primary Key, Candidate Key & Unique Key is the Super Key. As a single column of Customer Table i.e ‘cust_id’ is sufficient to identify the tuples uniquely from the table. Any set of the column which contains ‘cust_aadhaar_number’, ‘cust_pan_number’ is a Super Key. Foreign Key#A foreign key is a column which is known as Primary Key in the other table i.e. A Primary Key in a table can be referred to as a Foreign Key in another table. Foreign Key may have duplicate & NULL values if it is defined to accept NULL values. Example: In the above given relational table, ‘cust_id’ is Primary Key in the Customer table but ‘cust_id’ in the Order table known as a ‘Foreign Key’. Foreign Key in a table always becomes the Primary Key on the other table. what is a superkey#the key that can make sure $superkey1 \ne superkey2 \rightarrow tuple1 \ne tuple2$ what is a key#proper subset of superkey foreign key#reference of other(or itself) table’s key constraint#can only add to table if the foreign key is exsit in it’s reference table integrity constraint#primary domain, and some rules that it have to obay catgorize#L#need to be include in primary key R#never needed to be include in primary key LR#sometimes need to be include in primary key N#DDL#CREATE TABLE Books (BookNo INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, SortNo CHAR(10), Title CHAR(30), Author CHAR(12), Price FLOAT, LoanNo INTEGER); VARCHAR(32); #fixed length CHAR; #variable length INTEGER; SMALLINT; NUMERIC; #precision & scale DECIMAL; DATE(year, month, day); TIME(hour, minute, second); NOT NULL can be use to force data input when adding a new tuple, so if a column have no exist or currently unknown is not convenient table schema#relation no null or duplicate allowed table#ALTER TABLE Books ADD new_col data_type constraint; DROP; ALTER COLUMN col_name data_type; DROP TABLE table_name [RESTRICT|CASCADE] UNIQUE(); # except for null no duplicate PRIMARY KEY(); # only one for each table, NOT NULL for every attribute from the list FOREIGN KEY(attribute-list) REFERENCES(table attribute-list) ON DELETE SET NULL ON DELETE if there is a null in a simple semantic, it will be ignoreand in pasha(strict) semantic so we can choose strict or simple in DBMS like mysql or postgre? view & temporary table#view are a pre-produce table, that comes from a existing table, which can save storage CREATE ASSERTION name CHECK CREATE VIEW AS CREATE GLOBAL TEMPORARY TABLE CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY TABLE DML#manipulating language insert#INSERT INTO name VALUES value-list INSERT INTO VALUES INSERT INTO delete#DELETE FROM name WHERE condition update#UPDATE name SET set-name WHERE condition QL#query language commands#SELECT#SELECT col_name1(,) (col_name2...) FROM tbl_name; The table like this contains only requested columns is called result set. id name type_1 type_2 1 Bulbasaur Grass Poison 2 Ivysaur Grass Poison 3 Venusaur Grass Poison DON’T use SELECT * in production data Unnecessary cost. Two table return same column name might lead to a crash. WHERE#SELECT col_name1(,) (col_name2...) FROM tbl_name WHERE condition; The condition is used to evaluate every row in the table. When a row is evaluated true, it will be contain in the result set. Conditions = >= <= > < <> not equal, sometimes != works too AND between two operators NOT pre-fix OR AS#SELECT col_name1(,) AS name1 (col_name2...) FROM tbl_name WHERE condition; AS can change the column name into alias in the result set.If you want to use space in a alias you need to use ‘’ to quota it. However, in MySQL “” is also compatible. IN = ANY#Test if the value inside column matches any one item in a list of values. SELECT col_name1 FROM tbl_name WHERE col_name1 IN ('A', 'B', ...); There can be more than this in the brackets, it can be a result set generate by another query. LIKE#Just like = but wildcart can be use SELECT col_name1 FROM tbl_name WHERE col_name1 LIKE '%a'; Wildcards SQL Regex % .* _ . BETWEEN#Restrain the value in a range SELECT col_name1, col_name2 FROM tbl_name WHERE col_name1 BETWEEN 20 AND 30; Does BETWEEN A AND B have to satisfied $A<B$ ? yes ORDER BY#Order the result set by the value of a specific column#SELECT * FROM tbl_name ORDER BY col_name ASC; By default it will be sort in ascending order, if you prefer descending order, add DESC after the col_name When the specific column has same value, a secondary column will be helpful#SELECT * FROM tbl_name ORDER BY col_name1 ASC, col_name2 DESC; this secondary won’t be any help unless there are rows that have same value in first column INSERT#Add a complete new row to the table# for a table like this id name country language 1 John US English 2 Sam UK English 3 Tom CAN French INSERT INTO tbl_name VALUES (4, 'Chen', 'Chinese'); A query like this will produce a table like this will cause a error column doesn’t match, cause there are 4 columns in table and only 3 values given in the query. INSERT INTO tbl_name VALUES (4, 'Chen', 'CN', 'Chinese'); A correct one like this turn the table into id name country language 1 John US English 2 Sam UK English 3 Tom CAN French 4 Chen CN Chinese Add a partial row in to the table#For the last table, command like this INSERT INTO tbl(id, name, language) VALUES (5, 'Yoki', 'Janpaness'); this will produce a table as follow id name country language 1 John US English 2 Sam UK English 3 Tom CAN French 4 Chen CN Chinese 5 Yoki NULL Japaneses Or the NULL could be the value you defined as DEFAULT at the beginning. UPDATE#Modify existing rows UPDATE table SET col_name1 = 'rabbit' WHERE col_name2 = 1; DELETE#Delete data from a table ==Caution:==DELETE FROM tbl_name; will delete all the data in the table! Advanced SQL#ROUND#ROUND(a,b) b needs to be a integer but it can be under 0 which means to round into 10’s list set bag# Features\type list set bag duplicate yes yes yes ordering fixed unordered no commands#DISTINCT#Remove duplicates GROUP BY#When there are columns you SELECT has different row numbers, it will get confused which to print. So this is when you use GROUP BYGROUP BY is to group rows into different subset base on column(s), and do aggregates against each subset. HAVING#==Caution:== Can’t use an aggregation in a WHERE filtering clause, because they are not yet computed.By using a HAVING clause instead can use an uncomputed aggregation for filtering. SUBSTRING()#SUBSTRING(value, a, b) will get the a-b part of the value, cut a part of the string. The count start at 0. So for example SUBSTRING('suggestions', 0, 5) is equal to 'sugges'. || or CONCAT#str_1 || str_2 will concatnate str_1 and str_2 CONCAT() in MySQL works the same. Or in MySQL just put 'str_1' 'str_2' together, MySQL will concatenate it automatically DAYDIFF() (MySQL only)#Caculate how many days are there from day A to day B. to do this in sqlite is to do SELECT CAST((julianday(day_A) - julianday(day B)) AS INT) AS days The date format is ‘yyyy-mm-dd’ INTO#SELECT DISTINCT col_name INTO newtable FROM table; Copy column(s) into a new table. CREATE TABEL newtable( SELECT DISTINCT col_name FROM table;) In MySQL this will do the same thing. INSERT INTO#INSERT INTO table( SELECT DISTINCT col_name FROM table;) Copy column(s) into exsiting table.==caution:== the column number must match LIMIT#LIMIT 20 OFFSET 10 Skip 10 and return 20 rows(11~30) INTERSECT#the intersect of two select results And UNION#the union of two select results Or ALL#All the rows in the inner query must satisfy the expression problems when using#time zone not correct#SHOW VARIABLES LIKE '%time_zone%'; #show current time zone setting by default is UTC SYSTEM set global time_zone = '+8:00'; #this will change the global time_zone varible, it will take effect in next session set time_zone = '+8:00'; #this only change the varible in this session flush privileges; #save the changes

posts.title: UNIX basic commands
posts.text: 基础命令#ls mkdir rm rmdir cd touch cat echo grep | > >> ln (-s) find <起始目录> <选项表达式> <条件匹配表达式> <动作表达式> # 选项表达式 -L 遇到符号链接，跟踪链接地址 -regextype (-regex/-iregex) -depth/-d -moount/-xdev 查找时不跨越文件系统 -maxdepth LEVEL 最大查找深度 # 条件匹配表达式 -name myfile '*myfile*' -iname 文件名含有 -amin access N minutes ago -atime access N days ago -cmin create N minutes ago -ctime create N days ago -mmin modify N minutes ago -mtime modify N days ago # 访问文件：vim/sh/bash/cat/more等操作会重置Access time # 读取文件属性：ls/stat/wc/du等操作不会重置Access time # 修改文件：会重置Modify time 和 Change time # 修改权限但不调整内容： 不会重置Modify time， 但会重置 Change time。 -used used in N minutes -newer -uid -gid -user -group -nouser 所属不在/etc/passwd -nogroup 所属组不在/etc/group -perm MODE 权限为MODE -perm -MODE 权限至少为MODE -perm /MODE或+MODE 有一个用户权限为MODE即可 -type b c d p f l s -links N 硬链接数量 -inum N inode为N -samefile NAME 查找与NAME文件具有相同inode的文件 -size -empty #动作表达式 -print -ls -prune -exec COMMAND {} \; -ok COMMAND {} \; # 用户管理 adduser addgroup usermod # 网络 ifconfig # down load a url/* wget -m -np -nH

posts.title: artifical intelligence
posts.text: search terms that used Approach Revised Approach algorithm DFS BFS Greedy Best-First Search A* Search minimax algorithm Knowledge terms that used mark {background-color:yellow;} Harvard CS50.3 note search# eg. driving route terms that used# term meaning agent the object who perform actions state action ACTION(s) the actions agent got in specific state s transition model RESULT(s, a) what’s the result of performing action a in state s state space all states that you can get from initial state goal test way to determine whether a state is a goal state path cost numerical cost associated with a given path solution sequence of actions that leads from initial state to a goal state to solve search problem we use node data structure Approach#Start with a frontier that contains the initial state.Start with an empty explored set.if the frontier is empty, then no solution. Otherwise, remove a node from the frontier. If that node contains goal state, return solution. Else expand node. Revised Approach#Start with a frontier that contains the initial state.Start with an empty explored set.if the frontier is empty, then no solution. Otherwise, remove a node from the frontier. If that node contains goal state, return solution. Add the node to the explored set. Expand node, add resulting nodes to the frontier only if they aren’t already in the frontier or the explored set algorithm# attribute DFS BFS can always find the optimal path Yes No average move cost less more As you can see, each of these two have drawbacks. So we take a look at what will a human think of this problem. DFS#graph TB Start(DFS) --> Move[Go straight until branch or end] Move --> BranchOrEnd{Branch or end?} BranchOrEnd -->|End| Goal{"Is it the goal?"} Goal -->|Yes| End(End search) Goal -->|No| TraceBack[Trace back to former branch] TraceBack --> Move BranchOrEnd -->|Branch| Pick[Pick a side randomly] Pick --> Move BFS#graph TB Start(BFS) --> Move[move each thread a step forward] Move --> SingleRoute{Is it a single route?} SingleRoute --> |Yes| Move SingleRoute --> |No| BranchOrEnd{Branch or end?} BranchOrEnd -->|End| Goal{"Is it the goal?"} Goal -->|Yes| End(End searches) Goal -->|No| ExitThread[Stop this thread] BranchOrEnd -->|Branch| OpenThread[Open threads for each choice] OpenThread --> Move In these algorithm, BFS and DFS can be done in any time, no information required. While for the GBFS, information are used, in this case, coordinate of current location and endpoint. Search methods that don’t use problem specific knowledge like BFS and DFS are uninformed search and GBFS are called informed search since it used problem specific knowledge. Greedy Best-First Search#definition#$index = h(n)$ Calculate the estimate distance of each node to the goal using heuristic distance $h(n)$ (in the course Manhattan distance [^MD] was used, but there were more options like Diagonal Distance[^DD], Euclidean Distance[^ED]) in advanced and always pick the neighbor with the smallest index number when making decisions. [^MD]: Only available while agent is allow to move vertically and horizontally. $d = \Delta X + \Delta Y$[^DD]: Only available while agent is allow to move diagonally. $d = D * (\Delta X + \Delta Y) + (D2 - 2 * D) * min(\Delta X, \Delta Y)$ where D2 is the cost between two diagonally nearby node[^ED]: Only available while agent can move toward any direction. $d = \sqrt{(\Delta X)^2 + (\Delta Y)^2}$ A* Search#definition#$index = h(n) + g(n)$ Add up the heuristic distance $h(n)$ and the steps needed to get there $s(n)$ of each position in advanced and always pick the neighbor with the smallest index number when making decisions. restrains#A* Search is a optimal search algorithm only when: $h(n)$ is admissible (never overestimates the true cost) $h(n)$ is consistent (for every node $n$ and successor $n’$ with step cost $c$, $h(n) ≤h(n’) + c$) minimax algorithm#This algorithm works very well when it comes to two player games like tik-tac-toe or go chess(aka. weiqi) Use different value to descript each end situation of the game. As for tik-tac-toe, O wins can be value -1, even can be value 0 while X wins can be value 1. And in this case the goal of players can be simplied into try to get a smaller value or a biger value. graph TB A[A big] --> B1[B small] A --> B2[B2 small] B1 --> C1[C1 big:0] B2 --> C2[C2 big:1] B2 --> C3[C3 big:-1] Ways to optimize the algorithm Alpha-beta Pruning Evaluation funciton Knowledge#terms that used# term meaning sentence an assertion about the world knowledge base a set of sentences known by a knowledge-based agent entailment if a entails b then whenever a is true b is also true inference the process of deriving new sentences form old ones NotAndOrImplicateBiconditional to be continued

posts.title: blockchain intro.md
posts.text: 基础理解# 术语 理解 区块 一段时间的账目 链 每个区块之间联系起来的数据 PoW共识 （挖矿）<br > 检查记账是否正确 网络节点 矿工，记账先生是共识机制选出的特定的网络节点 P2P网络 互相通信以保障区块的不可篡改 共识机制 选举记账先生，给予记账先生手续费与报酬 分布式账本 没有哪个是权威，共同维护数据可靠性 遥感数据存储#IPFS#IPFS#Inter-planetary file system 分布式点对点文件系统，可以实现类似git的版本控制，网络会删除重复文件，并对每个文件进行版本历史追踪，每个文件都会生成对应的哈希值用于智能合约 好处# 易于分布：分布式文件存储协议，所以对于不同节点来说都是易于访问的 ==仅需储存单实例即可保障安全性==（估计是类似与raid那种可以通过校验值还原数据） 低带宽需求：点对点传输，可以通过IPFS寻址共享数据，缩短带宽需求（不需要中转服务器），加快数据共享速度 归档：不可改变数据存储，安全性保障 安全#目前常采用基于可信第三方的信任机制 （中心化） 分布式节点间的信任关系 服务商可以存在越权行为，滥用用户在云端的数据 基于区块链的PKI#原有CA采用集中式PKI（公钥管理基础设施）# 容易被中间人攻击非对称加密公钥发布过程无法确定是否是正确的目标对象发送的，可能是中间人发送的 单点故障可能导致大规模证书的不能使用 基于公共总帐建立PKI能够建立分布式PKI# 多点认证提高中间人攻击难度 提高数据存放份数，避免单点故障问题 证书签发透明实例Certcoin# 每个区块的merkle root涵盖三个内容（identity, public key, action: registration/update/revocation） PB-PKI# 基于区块链的PKI的改进 key-update方法与Certcoin不一样，PB-PKI不把之前的公钥和id上链 总结共识算法（优先），智能合约#可以从医学数据、农产品了解 证明类#工作量证明（PoW）#权益证明（Proof of Stake，PoS）#授权股权证明（DPoS）#权威证明(Proof of Authority，PoA)#贡献证明（Proof of Contribution，PoC）#拜占庭类#拜占庭算法 raft 不支持容错作恶节点，容错1/2#实用拜占庭算法 pbft 支持容错作恶节点，容错小于1/3#Algorand#Tendermint#燃烧证明、存在证明(Proof of Existence，PoE)[29]、数据可恢复证明（Proof of Retrievability，PoR）[30]、存储证明（Proof of Storage）[31] DAG类#IOTA#Byteball#结合云计算#哈希值，追溯#数据存储和分享#医疗领域应用#使用区块链安全地分享CT图像供深度学习训练使用 安全性#本地进行模型训练，然后将权重通过智能合约分享 准确性#使用==BAT算法==和==数据扩增==减少噪点和==过拟合==的出现 运作原理#把图像进行本地模型训练，把权重信息储存在IPFS中，然后再把IPFS的hash值和权重值存进区块链账目中深度学习处理本地模型数据，将处理后的权重存进IPFS，hash值存进区块链账目 https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/10.1007/s41870-020-00568-9.pdf http://webvpn.swu.edu.cn/https/77726476706e69737468656265737421e7e056d234336155700b8ca891472636a6d29e640e/science/article/pii/S0895611120301075

posts.title: computer network
posts.text: 要学什么 internet 需要什么 jargon what is the internet network edge network core routing table datagrame(udp) computer network packet old time packets look like this. new pockets layering feature example why layering? encapsulate upper layers don’t have to worry about what is the next layer. example compare VPN packet and a normal one byte order when programming a network application ipv4 example net mask address structure Classless Inter-Domain Routing(CIDR) longest prefix match(algorithm) ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) TCP service model reliable end2end bidirection 3 hand shakes transmit in segments teardown the connection summary congestion control timeout 3 duplicate ACKs TCP Tahoe always sets cwnd to 1 (timeout or 3 duplicate acks) IP SDN(software designed network) ATM(面向连接的三层交换机) router filter MAC multiple access links protocols MAC address and ARP ethernet others security cryptography and its many uses beyond “confidentiality” authentication message integrity availability firewalls and intrusion detection systems security in application, transport, network, link layers review 都是大题 要学什么# 电子设备中间传递信息 怎么实现 internet 需要什么# 设施 服务 协议 jargon# 英文 中文 transmission 网卡放01代码速度 host(end systems) 终端 switch 交换机（通常指数据链路层交换机） router 路由器（网络层） path =route 路径 package switch 把传输数据分段后发送 secret switch transmission rate protocol 语义 语法 时序 format, syntax(order), actions taken frame 网卡实现package到frame package 软件封包 Web VoIP email games e-commerce social nets client server physical media 传输媒介 network core routers, switch, network of networks core switch distribution switch access switch ITU 国际电信联盟 RFC Request For Comment 协议认定组织 IEDF ISP Internet Service Providers TCP Transmission Control Protocol UDP User Datagram Protocol ICMP Internet Control Message Protocol (ping) IP Internet Protocol SMTP Simple Mail Transfer Protocol SNMP Simple Network Management Protocol IMAP Internet Message Access Protocol POP Post Office Protocol HTTP Hyper Text Transfer Protocol DNS Domain Name System protocol FTP File Transport Protocol DHCP Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol ARP/RARP Address Resolution Protocol RIP PPP sends packet of data主要受限因素，网卡数据转化01速度，网线传输时间忽略不计packet transmission delay = time needed to transmit L-bit packet into link = $\frac{L(bits)}{R(bits/sec)}$ delay comes from transmission time consuming and queueing time consuming lost comes from no buffer in any hop switch 只关心物理和数据链路层router 关心物理层数据链路层和网络层 what is the internet#network edge#network core# mesh of interconnected routers packet-switching: store-and-forward routing table# base on routing algorithms to calculate the dynamic routing table PDU | protocol data unit|协议头加内容 datagrame(udp)# 无连接 不可靠 顺序无法保障 computer network#packet#old time packets look like this.# content header xxxxxxx des C B A The data transport form source to A, and when A find B is the next hop in the header and send it to B. Eventually the packet will be sent to the destination. And since all the hops are list in the packet that every router can see it, that is unsafe. So they come up with a new one. new pockets# When shake hands and open connection, all the routers will record where to send if the destination is this one in a table like this. destination where to send des B So the pockets will only contains the destination. And when router receive a pocket it go look up it in the table and sent it to the next hop. layering#feature# only communicate with the layer above or below. a massage goes form the toppest layer(application layer) all the way to the bottom(physic layer), and then gose all the way to the top again to reach the destination. example# edit (source code) compile (object code) link (executable) execute why layering?# modularity well defined service reuse separation of concerns continuous improvement p2p communications encapsulate#upper layers don’t have to worry about what is the next layer.#example# when the tcp change it’s algorism http don’t need to worry about how to change into tcp, they can work both on the old tcp and the new one. compare VPN packet and a normal one# wireless frame/ip/tcp/tls/ip/tcp/http wireless frame/ip/tcp/http byte order# byte order big endian small endian order the first byte place in the first address the last byte place in the first address use in what cpu amd intel arm when programming a network application# remember how many time you convert or you will be facing lots of bugs. ipv4# 32 bit long example# a.b.c.d (each letter represent a number from 0-255 which is an octect of binary number) an octect is a 8 digits number net mask# which ranger of ip are local that don’t need to go through a router when you send a package and whenever the netmask is 1 the corresponding binary digits of you ip address and desitination address are the same you are in the same local network. address structure# Class A: first bit is 0 and next 7 bit is network and last 24 is hosts Class B: first 2 bit is 1 0 and next 14 bit is network and last 16 is hosts Class C: first 3 bit is 1 1 0 and next 21 bit is network and last 8 is hosts Classless Inter-Domain Routing(CIDR)# use slash and a number to descibe the length of the prefix(netmask) /16 means the netmask is 16 bit of binary number which left 16 bit which is 65536 ipv4 addresses under the domain. similarly, /24 describes 256 addresses and /20 describes 4096 address. longest prefix match(algorithm)# it is a algorithm to decide how to forward a packet if a packet arrives and there isn’t a more specific match in the forwarding table than the default one, it will sent it through the default one. forwarding table is a set of CIDR entries. an address can match multiple entries. base on prefix length to decide which is more specific. ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)# only works in local network every node keep a cache of mapping of IP addresses to the link layer addresses(MAC). When the node need to send a packet to an IP address: If it has it in the cache it send it directly.(jump to 4) If it does not have the cache it will broadcast a request that ask “who have IP x.x.x.x tell x.x.x.x(itself) at xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx(MAC)” The node that have the specific IP will respond “I hve network address x.x.x.x and my link layer addresses is xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx”(not broadcast). And to the rest nodes in the network when they overhear the request, they can insert or refresh their ARP mapping in the cache. The node that send the request will generate a mapping of link layer addresses and IP addresses. TCP service model#reliable# Make sure every segment is well received without missing. Flow-control prevents overrunning receiver.(tells the sender how many room in receiver’s buffer) end2end#bidirection#3 hand shakes# SYN (synchronize) Send along with a number that identified where to start the byte-stream. SYN+ACK (acknowledge) Acknowledge of agreeing to communicate with it. And also send a SYN back for reverse direction connection. ACK Acknowledge of agreeing to the reverse direction connection. SYN# synchorize the data ACK# ACK=n means the packages before n is received and ready to receive n retransmission# timeout duplicates ACKs(3 duplicates) will trigger retransmission transmit in segments#teardown the connection#FIN (finish)# No longer have data to send. (Data)+ACK# Send the data before closing. And acknowledge that you are going to close the connection. FIN# When finish the rest of data that need to transmit, send a FIN to tell that I have done sending data. You can close now. ACK# The connection is now closed. ACK, checksum, sequence-number summary# e2e reliable data transfer syntax time order communitive ack flow control congestion control 3 hand shake & 4 hand wave Unit 3 home work:R 1,7,8,11,14,15,18P 3,7,8,18,26,31,40,44 congestion control#timeout#cwnd set to 1 MSS 3 duplicate ACKs#cut in half TCP Tahoe always sets cwnd to 1 (timeout or 3 duplicate acks)# 3.51 IP#SDN(software designed network)#A central cluster automatically calculate the best ip table for each router and continuously renew them. ATM(面向连接的三层交换机)#router filter# high-speed switching fabric(硬件交换，不需要处理数据，类似PLC)二进制位比较，先去掉掩码长度的头部，遍历选择最优路线（匹配位数最长的出口） MAC#multiple access links protocols#p2p ppp collision(碰撞) simultaneous transmission by nodes on single shared broadcast channel Master Slave, 轮询 pass token one by one, who got the token can use the channel Random compete the channel switch store and forwarding MAC address and ARP#MAC address is 64bit ARP is a protocol, know ip, want to know MACRARP = reverse ARPARP table is MAC to IP 波特率（每秒可以传输多少码元）/带宽 ethernet#preamble 7 bytes of 10101010 followed by one 10101011 others# CRC 校验(used in MAC layer) multiple access protocols security#cryptography and its many uses beyond “confidentiality”#f4 并行算法 authentication#message integrity#availability#firewalls and intrusion detection systems#firewall: filter application layer, transportation layer, network layer, link layerIDS: analyze data security in application, transport, network, link layers#review# 考前六章 每个协议的内容：语义 语法 时序都是大题# 问答题 综合题 算法题（dv， link-stat迪杰斯特拉） 实验题（路由表，分配端口，ip，启动端口） 体系结构（5层） 6.7 web encapsulate 子网划分 因特网架构 （子网掩码，网段划分 路由配置、算法 距离向量算法（chapter 5 p256） 区别 路由器 交换机 工作层级 网络层 数据链路层 处理的协议数据单元（pdu） package data frame 局域网交换 按照ip转发 按照mac地址转发

posts.title: learn to use git
posts.text: git [toc] Jargon# Treefolder Blobfile Working treeWhere all files exist dose it contains files that is not added? Staging area(Index)What added and are going to be contain in next commit will be in the staging area RepositoryDatabase that stores the history of the project basic use#Tree status# Dirty/modifiedNone of the modified files have been added for next commit StagedSome of the files have been added and can be commit now CommittedChange have been stored safely in a snapshot(commit) File status# TrackedWere part of last snapshot or newly added to the stage modified: have changed but not yet added unmodified: haven’t changed staged: have changed and add to stage or newly added to the stage UntrackedNot part of last snapshot nor are in the staging area commands#git commit#git commit -a include all the tracked files including modified but not staged ones git commit -m add a commit message in the commit command rather than use a default editor to write one git stash#To store dirty files in a special stack git stash to store all dirty files git stash list show previous stashes git stash apply apply latest stash git stash apply stash@{num} apply specific stash git stash drop same as apply only clear the stash instead of applying git stash pop same as apply only drop it after apply it git stash clear clear all the stashes git merge#Merge two branches git fetch#To get the remote branches that not on local repository git pull#Combination of git fetch and git merge git push#Push a branch to remote git push <remote> <local branch>:<rename as> push a brach to remote but use a different name git branch#git branch -u <remote>/<remote branch> set up to stream with remote branch git rebase#Redo the changes made to one branch since the branching to the another branch. This will make the changes are made in series not in parallel git remote#git remote add origin [git@url:port]:path/project.git # change the remote HEAD of origin to develop branch git remote set-head origin develop # set default push branch git branch --set-upstream-to=origin/mac git checkout#git checkout change into the situation of a commit-b create a new branch and change into it git diff#git diff diff a file between different commit Branching change and merging#If meeting merge conflict, try to automatically fix it, if can’t, report it.

posts.title: linux可以访问硬盘的情况下修改密码
posts.text: 适用于树莓派# 挂载内存卡(最好使用linux系统root用户) 修改/etc/ssh/sshd.conf#AuthorizedKeysFile .ssh/authorized_keys取消注释将路径改为你要放公钥的路径 将生成公钥，放在上述路径 ssh user@url即可登陆树莓派 由于树莓派sudo默认不需要密码，sudo su切换至root用户然后passwd user修改密码即可 sudo需要密码# 用包管理器安装whois(用于sha-512加密，可以用类似软件替代) 挂载内存卡(最好使用linux系统root用户) 查看/etc/shadow中要修改的用户一行 类似tom:$6$LyVpT/aetS$mTDFTG9SzM7iVdP7Kqz6q2lsvgqjA45qXDAEcIUV/eEAaOGreCbd1XMet8itx7z1XM1ZFCb3UUd3toBozQiuZ.:17599:0:99999:7::: 第一个第二个:之间的是加密后的密码，第一个和第二个$之间的是加密种类，第二个第三个之间是salt，第三个第四个之间是密文。 用whois中的mkpasswd -m sha-512 -S salt也可以不输入-S 和 salt直接使用一个新的salt 会提示输入密码，这个即为你要设置的新密码 用生成的字符串替换第一个和第二个:之间的字符即可 /etc/目录下可能有shadow的备份，我遇到的是shadow-，也需要一并修改

posts.title: UNIX cli tools comparing
posts.text: 搜索 fg 结果整理 rg sed awk cut 比较 diff cmp sdiff wdiff 网络工具 ifconfig mntui 搜索#find# 实时扫描 fg# 比find更快，用数据库，类似于windows下的everything 结果整理#rg# 搜索工具,比grep快,可以搜索流或者文件内容 sed# 按正则匹配筛选 awk# 按字符块输出, 支持正则筛选 cut#比较#diff# 比较文件或目录cmp# 比较两个文件并且定位每个不同的位置sdiff# 比较文件并且可以合并wdiff# 一个单词一个单词比较不同 网络工具#ifconfig# ifconfig eth0(device) 192.168.10.2(given ip) netmask 255.255.255.0 broadcast 192.168.10.255 up(turn on)mntui# 命令行ui，适合在没有DE的情况下配置wifi，依赖NetworkManager。

posts.title: server building
posts.text: configurations system installing service running/been ran on it configurations# processor i3-7100T35w Since servr have to run 7*24, it should be power-saving. Otherwise it would be a large eletricity bill to pay. motherboard 昂达 B365SD3-ITX This is really not the best motherboard to use, DDR3 runs at a lower speed and when compared to DDR4 it only have slight price adventage. But I have two 4g DDR3 RAM, so I choice this to suit with 7 gen cpu and DDR3 at the same time. RAM DDR3 4g * **2 镁光 power supply 300w case 银欣ITX cooling system IDCOOLING IS-40X 4 heatpipePHANTEKS MG SKIRON 12cm hard drive 2.5mm 2T Seagate SMR (quit using as soon as possible, when I can afford two PMR to build a raid array) Toshiba 4T * 2 5400rpm (raid 1) ssd 杂牌64G SATA（when SMART report it was danger to continue using, I change it into the Lexar I’m using now） Lexar nm100 SATA 128G system installing# Ubuntu 20.04LTS (place in the living room used both as a media player for my parents and a 24*7 was) OMV (when I found my parents seldom use it, I change into OMV for more convenient raid experience) FreeNAS (freeBSD need VM to run docker, when you are using a i3 processor it really drives people crazy) OMV (yes, I’m back) TrueNAS scale (useable ZFS+ Debian + docker, this is the dream NAS OS) service running/been ran on it# Nextcloud (appreciate by my mum so she don’t need to use WeChat or flash drive to keep her documents synchronised) Jellyfin (use aria2 to download movie and Jellyfin to manage them is a good choice) No-ip (DDNS + Dynamic external IP is a solution to access it when you are at school, though expose you NAS can be danger for you data, so make sure you know what you are doing) PhotoPrime (google open-source gallery, use tenser flow to classify photos, after using iCloud this had been abandoned) Aria2 pro (best aria2 docker ever found, move when complete, auto update tracker-list and auto delete temp file when complete)

posts.title: shell basic
posts.text: Job Control#stop# Ctrl-C (cancel SIGINT) Ctrl-\ (SIGQUIT) kill pause# Ctrl-Z (SIGSTOP) fg bg (still children of current terminal) & suffix (run a job in background and print all the output which can be annoying) nohup (start a job that live beyond the terminal) disown (not longer own one job, so it won’t be kill when you exit the terminal) use return value as a part of latter command# outer command \ inner command` Command editing# !$ last argument !:1 the first argument from last command !:1-2 Alt + . look for previous arguments

posts.title: nmap
posts.text: what is nmap#conclusion of what can nmap does: host discovery port discovery/ enumeration service discovery OS version detection MAC address detection service version detection (eg. apache nginx mysql) vulnerability/ exploit detection, using namp scripts nmap IDS/ portscan deteciton simple commands#namp -sP 192.168.0.0/16#/16 scan from 192.168.0.1 - 192.168.254.254-sP stands for ping scan namp -p 1-1000 -Pn -sV -sS -T4 target#-p 1-1000 specify port 1-1000-sV service version detecitonT4 scaning speed(1-5), larger number usully fast but could lead to inaccurate.-sS SYN scanPn treat all host online nmap -v -sS -A -T4 target#-v verbose output-A ALL usually quite slow (include OS and version detection + traceroute and scripts against target services) namp -O -sV -sS -T5 target#-O OS and version detection nmap -iL ip-address.txt#scan IPs from files nmap -p- target#nmap scan all ports nmap -oG grep-output.txt target#oG grepable output nmap target --webxml -oX - | xsltproc --output file.html -#output a HTML report

posts.title: ubuntu power saving
posts.text: 优化原理#优化cpu调度#tlp#使用tlpuiAC/BAT调度模式分别设置为schedutil和powersave关闭BAT状态下的boostAC/BAT性能策略为default/balance_power 禁用不必要服务项及应用#systemctl #查看所有服务项 systemctl #stop 查看临时禁用是否有影响 systemctl #disable 禁用开机启动 systemctl #mask 阻止关联启动 kill $(pidof {the programe you want to stop}) # 我禁用了compton(窗口渲染器) 限制进程cpu资源分配# cpulimitcpulimit -e {nameOfProgram} -l 30 # -l 的最大值是cpu线程数

posts.title: 正则表达式
posts.text: 为啥要学正则表达式#泛用性好#其实正则表达式的应用面很广，比如我个人比较喜欢使用的编辑器vim里面就原生支持了/ :s两种可以用正则表达式来高效的搜索、替换的方法。现代的高级语言如python, golang, java基本都有原生支持正则表达式。一些搜索引擎也有类似语法可以用于精确搜索。虽然不同软件的支持有一些默认上的差距 比如vim里面+默认是当作字符而不是关键字+的，python里面默认匹配是贪婪的(当少数或多数都可以满足匹配式的情况下选择多数的)，但是往往切换的学习成本是很小的 高效#可以实现一些字符串搜索不能完成的事情 比如 我想搜索一位或多位数字+一个 . 构成的小标题，如果使用字符串搜索会类似%d.不同的软件使用的语法不一定一样 而且这种表述只能搜索到小标题1-9，两位数的小标题就搜不出来了。因此，在进行批处理对时候(批量修改word内容，批量修改文件名)的时候正则表达式就好显著的提升效率。 常用语法# \d 所有数字 \D 所有非数字 [abc] a或b或c [\^abc] 匹配除了a,b,c以外的所有 . 所有字符 \. 转义,句号 [a-z] [A-Z] [0-9] 某一range内所有，只匹配一个字符, 大小写敏感 {3} 之前的正则字符重复三次 {3,6} 之前的正则字符三次到六次, 省略一个数字可以表示到0或者到无穷 * 0～$\infty$ 个任意字符(可以与别的表达式组合表达次数) + 1～$\infty$ 个任意字符(可以与别的表达式组合表达次数) ? 在字符后面跟一个?表示这个字符是可有可无的 ␣ 空格\t tab\n 换行\r windwos换行是\n\r\s 任意数量任意种类的空白字符 ^ $ 行首行尾，不匹配字符 ^(IMG\d+)\.png$ 匹配但是只获取其中一部分，可以嵌套多个括号，从上一步的结果获取其中更小的一部分 (?:) 类似于匹配 但是实际上没有捕获括号括起来的组，相当于整合一个组。 可以用于(?:\d\s)* 让*作用与和\d\s | 或
